title: uniface.ValidationError
text: uniface.ValidationError The uniface.ValidationError object provides access to the error number and message of a validation error on the server. It is returned by the FieldErrorContext.getError function when used in the showError and clearError web triggers. Syntax Error Properties Property Name Description message Validation error message. valueInError Field value that caused the validation error. Description A uniface.ValidationError object is generated when validation fails on the server after data has been submitted from the client. You can access the properties of the uniface.ValidationError using the getError function of the field. Example: Using uniface.ValidationError For example, in the following code, the showError web trigger is used to display an alert box with the information about the error. The example performs the following: Gets the ValidationError object using the field's getError function. Extracts the elements of the error object and put them into the variable vErrorString . Displays an alert with contents of the vErrorString variable. Returns true to enable the default Uniface error handling. Explain this code webtrigger showError javascript // this = the current FieldErrorContext object var vErrorObject = this.getError(); 1 var vErrorString = "There is a Validation Error./n/n"; 2 vErrorString += "Value in error: " + vErrorObject.valueInError+ "/n/n"; alert(vErrorString); // Show error alert box 3 endjavascript return true 4 end Copy Related Links getError() Returns an error object for the field, if a syntax error or validation error occurred. Syntax Checking in Dynamic Server Pages Uniface always performs server-side syntax checks when data in a dynamic server page (DSP) is validated or stored. It is also possible to perform browser-side syntax checks, so that errors are flagged before incorrect data is submitted to the server. webtrigger onSyntaxError The onSyntaxError web trigger is activated when a field syntax violation is detected in the browser. It can be used to customize or override the default syntax error handling. webtrigger onSyntaxErrorResolved The onSyntaxErrorResolved web trigger is used to customize behavior when a field syntax error has been corrected.
